The person was then asked to list up to 6 thoughts that crossed their mind while playing as the receiver and they answered: "be fair, send even amount.; don't look like a sap; be giving; follow the golden rule.; be kind; Don't forget to be a nice person."
column147: "being fair; not being stupid; looking out a bit for myself.; worry about what other people think.; offering something to other."
column148: "I aspire to be more patient. with my health concerns, and getting older, I need more help. I have to be kinder to those around me. I'm starting to realize that I NEED their help. They are hanging out with me because they care for me. I have to embrace this."
column149: "I want to be someone patient, nice, kind and giving. I want to model good behavior for others. I want to make friends, and I ought to bebetter at it. I ought to be more giving to others, and show them how I feel."
column150: "I'm imperfect, I don't give enough. I lose my cool. When someone offers to help my immediate reaction is to refuse it, and insist on doing itmyself. I need to stop that. It's unwise and silly. I need help."

column148: "I aspire to be someone that others enjoy being around. I would want to be known for different characteristics for that to happen- being a good listener, being humble, and using my words to affirm them. I would also endeavor to be humble and hard working, and known for being someone that others can depend on"
column149: "The type of person that I ought to be is the best version of myself. column120: "The greatest potential is to share all.; I hope they decide to be fair and repay my generosity.; It doesn't pay to be selfish.". column148: "The ideal me would be a bit more daring and adventurous. I would balance enjoying life in the moment with planning for the future. I would be a little more forgiving and understanding when others make mistakes. I would be more willing to face my weaknesses and address them head on instead of trying to avoid them."
column149: "I should take care of the needs of my family. Most importantly, my elderly mother needs extra care, which will only increase in the future. In addition, my disabled daughter will need constant care throughout her lifetime as well. Whatever my own needs are, I ought to prioritize the needs of the people in my life who need me and rely on me for care and support. I need to be selfless and giving of my time and energy, willing to sacrifice for them."
column150: "I believe I am selfless. I do what I need to do for my family, although I sometimes resent having to do it. I have some deep rooted fears from my childhood that I've never faced, and as I get older, I fear it is harder to face them. I worry a lot and sacrifice much for the future of myself and my family, thus sometimes over-sacrificing enjoyment of life now for future security. I worry a little more than most people about possible future scenarios."

column150: "I believe I am selfless. I do what I need to do for my family, although I sometimes resent having to do it. I have some deep rooted fears from my childhood that I've never faced, and as I get older, I fear it is harder to face them. I worry a lot and sacrifice much for the future of myself and my family, thus sometimes over-sacrificing enjoyment of life now for future security. I worry a little more than most people about possible future scenarios."
